title: Ownership model, migration, and legacy-owner backfill
description: Add projects.owner_user_id (nullable FK, additive migration). Items, listing_matches, alerts_sent, and the new project-scoped decision/feedback table (FEATURE-1034) remain owned transitively via their existing FK to projects/items. Catalogue and listing tables (products, product_price_observations, product_new_price_history, product_price_candidates, listings, listing_identities, listing_duplicates, auction_snapshots, source_runs) get NO ownership column and are not touched by this migration — they stay platform-owned/shared. On first boot after this migration, backfill existing projects to a single deterministically-created legacy-owner user.
acceptance_criteria: projects.owner_user_id added via the existing additive _MIGRATIONS pattern in db.py, idempotent on repeated connect()
No user_id/owner column is added to items, listing_matches, alerts_sent — ownership is resolved transitively via their existing FK chain to projects

No user_id/owner column is added to products, product_price_observations, product_new_price_history, product_price_candidates, listings, listing_identities, listing_duplicates, auction_snapshots, or source_runs — these remain platform-owned/shared with no ownership concept at all
Migration creates exactly one legacy-owner user record and assigns it as owner_user_id for all pre-existing projects, with no manual operator step required
Running the migration twice (idempotency) does not create a second legacy-owner user or reassign already-owned projects
A pre-migration database fixture, migrated in a test, ends up with zero NULL owner_user_id rows on projects afterward
notes: See docs/adr/0004-user-owned-data-model-and-migration.md (revised 2026-07-08 — catalogue/listing tables explicitly excluded from ownership after a product-boundary sanity check). owner_user_id IS NULL is not a supported steady state on projects after migration completes; it never exists on catalogue/listing tables at all.
